body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
July 8, 2026
Stablecoin market cap hit a record $322B in June (+29% YTD), while H1 2026 hack losses fell below $1B despite a record number of incidents.
Stablecoin News
Open USD (OUSD) launches with 140+ partners
Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, Coinbase and BlackRock backed the June 30 launch of a zero-fee-mint stablecoin; news of the launch sent Circle's stock down 16%.
USDC volume overtakes Tether
Circle's USDC hit record trading volume of $1.79T, pulling ahead of Tether for the first time in this metric as competition in stablecoin rails intensifies.
MiCA deadline triggers largest EU delisting wave
The EU's MiCA transition period ended July 1; Coinbase, Kraken and Crypto.com restricted or delisted USDT for EU users, the biggest European stablecoin delisting event on record.
GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline nears
Six U.S. federal agencies have until July 18 to publish the finalized regulatory framework for permitted payment stablecoin issuers.
Hacking & Security Incidents
BONK DAO governance hijack drains $20M
On July 6 an attacker bought voting power and pushed through malicious proposal BIP #76, draining ~5% of BONK's total token supply from the community treasury.
KelpDAO exploit among H1's biggest at $294M
The KelpDAO breach ranks as one of the two largest crypto hacks of H1 2026, together with the Drift Protocol hack accounting for nearly 59% of all funds stolen this year.
Same-block backrun extraction costs trader $2M
GoPlus Security flagged a same-block price-discrepancy exploit where a trader swapping 1,126 ETH received a fraction of the expected token value.
Crypto hack count hits record high in H1 2026
Incident frequency reached a record in H1 2026, though total losses fell sharply below $1B, suggesting smaller average payouts per attack.
